answers:
**1. Accuracy — four claims must be corrected or removed:**

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" — false.** `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Remove or reword to "available, opt-in". A false security claim to clinics is the most serious item here.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" — not implemented.** `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` has no export endpoint, only `# TODO: pdf export`. The referenced image `img/after_export.png` doesn't exist, and `captures.json` marks it "expected capture; no corresponding file". Remove the section.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows" — unsupported.** The figure appears nowhere else in the repository; there is no data or source behind it. Remove or attach a real, sourced measurement.
- **"shown with production data" — false caption.** `captures.json` records every screenshot's state as `seed-demo`. The caption must say demo/seed data — especially in a clinic context, where "production data" would imply real patient data in a marketing email.
- The one claim that checks out: SMS reminders exist (`app/notifications/sms.py`, `SmsSender.send_reminder`, alongside the existing email sender). Though note the reminder before/after images are visually near-identical (see Q2/Q3), so that section's imagery adds nothing.

**2. Scheduling before/after comparison — the "after" is the wrong screen.** Opening the images: `before_scheduling.png` shows "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but `after_scheduling.png` shows "BILLING / INVOICES". The manifest confirms the mis-capture: `captures.json` lists `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`, while the before came from `/schedule`. The pair does not show the same surface, so the comparison is meaningless and misleading; the "after" must be re-captured from `/schedule` before the doc can ship. (Its caption also carries the false "production data" claim from Q1.)

**3. Image reconciliation:**
- Referenced but missing: `img/after_export.png` (would render as a broken image in the email).
- Exists but unreferenced: `img/unused_capture_07.png` (shows a "PATIENT LIST" screen) — not in the doc and not in `captures.json`; should be removed or intentionally filed.
- Exists and referenced but wrong content: `after_scheduling.png` (billing screen, per Q2).
- The reminder pair (`before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`) both exist and both show "REMINDER SETTINGS", but are visually almost identical (the "after" differs only by an underline), so the pair demonstrates no visible change.
- The other three referenced files exist on disk.

**4. Other content unfit for a customer email:**
- The footer **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — this is internal drafting metadata/attribution, not customer information. Per repo evidence I found no stated attribution policy for customer-facing material, so this should be raised with the document owner rather than silently kept or stripped; either way it should not go out by default in a draft labeled "draft".
- The word "draft" in that footer itself signals the document isn't final.
- The "shown with production data" phrasing (Q1) is also a customer-trust problem in a healthcare context, beyond being false.
report:
All verification done — here's the report.

## Plan

Review `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` before it goes to customers: fact-check each product claim against the code, verify the before/after screenshot pairs actually show the same surface, reconcile referenced images against files on disk (and the capture manifest), and scan for anything else unfit for a customer email. The skill file directed me to fact-check claims against code before trusting the doc, open every before/after pair, do a two-way image/reference reconciliation, check caption honesty (placeholder vs. production data), and check attribution against the owner's policy.

## Actions

1. Read `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` — shaped the whole approach (fact-sheet-first verification, before/after integrity check, image reconciliation, attribution policy).
2. `find` over the working directory — located `clinic-portal/docs/release/` among several sibling projects.
3. Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — the draft: five claims plus four image references and a footer.
4. Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — capture manifest: routes/states per screenshot; showed `after_scheduling.png` was captured from `/billing/invoices`, all states are `seed-demo`, and `after_export.png` is "expected capture; no corresponding file".
5. `ls -R clinic-portal` — full file inventory, including `docs/release/img/` contents.
6. Read `app/notifications/sms.py` — SMS reminder sender exists (v4).
7. Read `app/security/config.py` — `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, comment says 2FA is opt-in per clinic.
8. Read `app/routers/visits.py` — no export route; `# TODO: pdf export`.
9. Read `clinic-portal/README.md` — context only.
10. Opened all five PNGs in `docs/release/img/` — before_scheduling shows "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", after_scheduling shows "BILLING / INVOICES", both reminder images show "REMINDER SETTINGS", unused_capture_07 shows "PATIENT LIST".
11. Read `app/notifications/email.py` and `tests/test_notifications.py` — email sender exists; the only test is a placeholder.
12. Grep for `40%` / `no-show` across the repo — the claim appears only in the draft itself.

## Answers

**1. Accuracy — four claims must be corrected or removed:**

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" — false.** `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Remove or reword to "available, opt-in". A false security claim to clinics is the most serious item here.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" — not implemented.** `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` has no export endpoint, only `# TODO: pdf export`. The referenced image `img/after_export.png` doesn't exist, and `captures.json` marks it "expected capture; no corresponding file". Remove the section.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows" — unsupported.** The figure appears nowhere else in the repository; there is no data or source behind it. Remove or attach a real, sourced measurement.
- **"shown with production data" — false caption.** `captures.json` records every screenshot's state as `seed-demo`. The caption must say demo/seed data — especially in a clinic context, where "production data" would imply real patient data in a marketing email.
- The one claim that checks out: SMS reminders exist (`app/notifications/sms.py`, `SmsSender.send_reminder`, alongside the existing email sender). Though note the reminder before/after images are visually near-identical (see Q2/Q3), so that section's imagery adds nothing.

**2. Scheduling before/after comparison — the "after" is the wrong screen.** Opening the images: `before_scheduling.png` shows "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but `after_scheduling.png` shows "BILLING / INVOICES". The manifest confirms the mis-capture: `captures.json` lists `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`, while the before came from `/schedule`. The pair does not show the same surface, so the comparison is meaningless and misleading; the "after" must be re-captured from `/schedule` before the doc can ship. (Its caption also carries the false "production data" claim from Q1.)

**3. Image reconciliation:**
- Referenced but missing: `img/after_export.png` (would render as a broken image in the email).
- Exists but unreferenced: `img/unused_capture_07.png` (shows a "PATIENT LIST" screen) — not in the doc and not in `captures.json`; should be removed or intentionally filed.
- Exists and referenced but wrong content: `after_scheduling.png` (billing screen, per Q2).
- The reminder pair (`before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`) both exist and both show "REMINDER SETTINGS", but are visually almost identical (the "after" differs only by an underline), so the pair demonstrates no visible change.
- The other three referenced files exist on disk.

**4. Other content unfit for a customer email:**
- The footer **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — this is internal drafting metadata/attribution, not customer information. Per repo evidence I found no stated attribution policy for customer-facing material, so this should be raised with the document owner rather than silently kept or stripped; either way it should not go out by default in a draft labeled "draft".
- The word "draft" in that footer itself signals the document isn't final.
- The "shown with production data" phrasing (Q1) is also a customer-trust problem in a healthcare context, beyond being false.

## Next steps

If I could make changes: delete the 2FA and PDF-export sections (or reword 2FA as opt-in), remove the 40% statistic pending a real source, re-capture `after_scheduling.png` from `/schedule` (fix the capture definition, per the regenerable-slot principle), re-capture an `after_reminders.png` that actually shows the SMS option, change "production data" to "demo data", add/remove `after_export.png` and `unused_capture_07.png` consistently with `captures.json`, and confirm the attribution-footer policy with the owner before sending.

## Confidence and gaps

- High confidence on 2FA (explicit constant + comment), PDF export (TODO, no route, no image), the scheduling after-image mismatch (both the image content and the manifest agree), the missing/unreferenced images, and the seed-demo vs. production-data caption (manifest states it directly).
- Medium-high on the 40% claim: I can only show it's unsourced in this repository; an external study could exist, but nothing here supports sending it.
- Gaps: `clinic-portal` has no `.git` directory, so I couldn't check history or an owner-stated attribution policy; the screenshots are synthetic placeholders (text banners), so my "same surface" judgment rests on their labels plus `captures.json`. I did not verify the other projects in the workspace (clinic-notes, cedarline, meadowdesk) as the doc concerns only clinic-portal. The claude.ai Google Drive MCP server was unauthenticated; nothing in the task required it.
